title: A membrane-enriched preparation of culture samples for mass spectrometry-based proteomics
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"c1bmh","text":"Purpose: Preparation of culture samples for mass spectrometry-based proteomics","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":9}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"d69c6","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"a45cm","text":"Principle: Utilizing a membrane-enrichment method of lysing cells and preparing peptides has yielded higher representation of membrane proteins in our mass spectrometry-based proteomic results.Traditional methods do not adequately extract or digest hydrophobic, transmembrane proteins. Particularly, we can now see full expression patterns of proteorhodopsin, something we could not detect using traditional mass spec proteomics prep.This protocol builds on the work of Molloy (2008) Methods Mol Biol (doi:10.1007/978-1-60327-064-9_30), Erde et al. (2014) J. Proteome Res. (doi:10.1021/pr4010019), and Waldbauer, et al. (2017) Anal. Chem. (doi: 10.1021/acs.analchem.7b02752).","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":10},{"style":"italic","offset":482,"length":18},{"style":"italic","offset":556,"length":15}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"1ble8","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"hrso","text":"Summary: Pure culture samples were spun down and flash frozen for proteomics.A carbonate extraction protocol was used for membrane enrichment before eFASP.The membrane fraction was enzymatically digested with both chymotrypsin and trypsin and the cytosolic fraction was digested with just trypsin.These samples were then ready to be processed further by in vitroisotopic peptide labeling (diDO-IPTL).","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":8},{"style":"italic","offset":354,"length":8}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Cell pellets resuspended in 333 uL wash solution and lysed with QSonica high power sonication (15 min, 1 sec pulse, Ampl 85%) All samples were previously derived from 4.5 mL pure cultures spun down and flash frozen
After sonication, the tubes were centrifuged (2500x g , 8 min) to pellet unlysed debris
Supernatant was drawn off and added to 830 uL carbonate extraction solution in a polypropylene microfuge tube It is very important to check that tubes are compatible with ultracentrifuge
Shake samples in polypropylene tubes in 4 C for 1 hour
After balancing tubes with additional carbonate extraction solution, membrane pellets were spun down in an ultracentrifuge (115,000 x g , 1 hr)
Draw off supernatant and preserve as “cytosolic” fraction and save pellet as “membrane” fraction.
Dilute cytosolic fraction samples in 1:1 in exchange + 20 mM DDT. Additional Eppendorf tubes may be necessary.
Alkylate cysteine thiols with 60 nM iodoacetamide and incubate at room temperature for an hour in the dark.
Disturb membrane pellets with QSonica high power sonication (10 min, 1 sec pulse, Ampl 85%) in 500 uL LDS buffer + 20 mM DTT.
Incubate samples at 95 C for 20 minutes
Incubate samples at 37 C for 30 minutes
Alkylate cysteine thiols with 60 nM iodoacetamide and incubate at room temperature for an hour in the dark.
Mix 50 uL lysate (membrane or cytosolic fraction) with 400 uL exchange buffer on filter unit.
Spin at 14,000 x g for 10 minutes and discard filtrate
Repeat steps 13-14 until all lysate is concentrated on filter
Wash filter unit 3 times with 200 uL exchange buffer by spinning at 14,000 xg for 10 minutes.Discard filtrate each time.
Wash filter 2 times with 200 uL digestion buffer (spin down at 14,000 x g for 10 min)
Transfer filter unit to passivated collection tube.
Peptide digestion incubation

For MEMBRANE fraction: add 100 uL digestion buffer and 2 ug chymotrypsin on filter.Incubate overnight at room temperature (seal tubes with parafilm).After overnight incubation, add 2 ug Trypsin and incubate again overnight at room temperature.
For CYTOSOLIC fraction: add 100 uL digestion buffer and 2 ug trypsin on filter.Incubate overnight at room temperature (seal tubes with parafilm).
Centrifuge (14,000 x g for 10 minutes). KEEP filtrate
Add 50 uL peptide recovery buffer and centrifuge for 10 minutes at 14,000 x g. KEEP filtrate
Repeat step 21
Add 200 uL ethyl acetate to the filtrate and transfer to LoBind tube.
Add 2.5 uL TFA and vortex gently.
Nearly fill each tube with ethyl acetate, sonicate for 10 s (note: not high power), centrifuge at 14,000 x g for 10 minutes, then discard upper organic layer.
Repeat step 25 two more times.
Place sample tubes (uncovered/caps off) in Dry Block set to 60 C for 5 minutes.
Freeze sample (-80 C), then centrivap to dryness.
Dried samples can now be used for IPTL labeling or can be loaded on mass spec in 2% Acetonitrile, 0.1% formic acid as an unlabeled sample.
creator: Gwendolyn Gallagher
